entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Arachis hypogaea, the target crop affected by crown rot

entities.name: CROWN ROT
entities.description: A disease of groundnut caused by Aspergillus niger, prevalent in warm and dry climatic zones

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS NIGER
entities.description: The phytopathogenic fungus that causes crown rot in groundnut

entities.name: WARM AND DRY CLIMATIC ZONES
entities.description: Environmental conditions where crown rot incidence is prevalent

entities.name: FUNGICIDES
entities.description: Chemical agents used for the management of crown rot

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL CONTROL
entities.description: A cost-effective and environmentally safe approach to managing plant diseases using biocontrol agents

entities.name: TRICHODERMA SPP
entities.description: Antagonistic fungi capable of controlling economically important diseases in several crop plants

entities.name: TRICHODERMA HARZIANUM
entities.description: A specific Trichoderma species tested for the biological control of crown rot in groundnut

entities.name: TRICHODERMA VIRIDE
entities.description: A specific Trichoderma species mentioned as a biological control agent for crown rot

entities.name: BACILLUS SUBTILIS AF 1
entities.description: A biocontrol agent tested for controlling crown rot and inducing plant defenses

entities.name: LIPOXYGENASE
entities.description: An enzyme whose production is induced by Bacillus subtilis AF 1 in groundnut seedlings

entities.name: PHYTOALEXIN METABOLISM
entities.description: A metabolic process in groundnut seedlings altered by Bacillus subtilis AF 1

entities.name: IN VITRO
entities.description: Laboratory-based testing of the antagonistic potential of Trichoderma isolates

entities.name: GREENHOUSE CONDITIONS
entities.description: Controlled environment used to test the efficacy of Trichoderma isolates against A. niger
